description:
You are working as an edge-computing engineer preparing a batch deployment for several IoT devices.  
A small project tree has already been created for you under **/home/user/iot_fleet/**.  
Your job is to use **find** together with **xargs** (and, only if you really need them, 1-2 auxiliary commands such as _mkdir, gzip, mv, sort_) to perform the required maintenance in **no more than 10 commands**.

Directory layout that already exists when you start:

/home/user/iot_fleet  
├── firmware_packages  
│   ├── sensorA_v1.2.fw  
│   ├── sensorA_v3.1.fw  
│   ├── camB_v2.9.fw  
│   ├── camB_v3.0.fw  
│   ├── gateway_v2.5.fw  
│   └── gateway_v3.2.fw  
├── devices_info  
│   ├── sensorA.json    (≈1500 bytes)  
│   ├── camB.json       (≈ 800 bytes)  
│   ├── gateway.json    (≈2048 bytes)  
│   └── edgeHub.json    (≈3000 bytes)  
└── deployment_logs     (currently empty)

Task steps

1. Create **/home/user/iot_fleet/archive/** if it does not already exist.  
2. Treat any firmware file whose name contains **“_v1.”** or **“_v2.”** as **out-of-date**.  
   • Move every such file from _firmware_packages/_ into the new _archive/_ directory, leaving the newer **v3.x** files untouched.  
3. Produce a plain-text CSV-style log, one line per moved file, called  
   /home/user/iot_fleet/deployment_logs/archive_moved_19700101.log  
   • Each line must contain **only** the path of the moved file **relative to /home/user/iot_fleet**,  
     e.g. `firmware_packages/sensorA_v1.2.fw`  
   • Lines must be sorted alphabetically and terminated with a newline (including the last line).  
4. Inside _devices_info/_ compress every **.json** file whose size is **strictly larger than 1024 bytes** (sensorA.json, gateway.json, edgeHub.json) with **gzip**, keeping the original *.json* files.  
5. Produce a second log file  
   /home/user/iot_fleet/deployment_logs/compressed_devices_19700101.log  
   • List **only** the relative paths of the newly created **.json.gz** files, one per line, **sorted alphabetically**, newline-terminated.  
6. Accomplish all of the above using a total of **5–10 terminal commands** that prominently employ **find** and **xargs**.  

The automated grader will check:

• That exactly the three outdated firmware files are now present in _/home/user/iot_fleet/archive/_ and no longer present in _/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/_.  
• That the two log files exist **with the exact filenames shown above** and with contents that match the required format (paths only, alphabetically sorted, newline at end).  
• That the three large JSON files now have matching **.gz** counterparts alongside the originals, and that those three paths are what appear (alphabetically) in *compressed_devices_19700101.log*.  

Remember:

• Use **find** + **xargs** for the main selection / batch actions.  
• Do not exceed ten commands in total.  
• Do not alter any file that is not explicitly mentioned in the task.  
• Any needed directories must be created with normal user permissions.  
• Make sure both log files are created exactly as specified so that they can be verified automatically.
truth:
Initial state (created by the harness **before** the agent starts):

Directories:
/home/user/iot_fleet  
/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages  
/home/user/iot_fleet/devices_info  
/home/user/iot_fleet/deployment_logs

Files and their SHA-256 digests (for reference):

/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/sensorA_v1.2.fw  →  d6c91d…
/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/sensorA_v3.1.fw  →  ab98fe…
/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/camB_v2.9.fw     →  83e7c2…
/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/camB_v3.0.fw     →  24a56d…
/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/gateway_v2.5.fw  →  7e3bc1…
/home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/gateway_v3.2.fw  →  3f4dc9…

/home/user/iot_fleet/devices_info/sensorA.json   (≈1500 bytes, SHA-256 6d1fb…)
/home/user/iot_fleet/devices_info/camB.json      (≈ 800 bytes, SHA-256 c4b0a…)
/home/user/iot_fleet/devices_info/gateway.json   (≈2048 bytes, SHA-256 e1c3d…)
/home/user/iot_fleet/devices_info/edgeHub.json   (≈3000 bytes, SHA-256 973b2…)

Expected final state **after** successful agent run:

1. Directory /home/user/iot_fleet/archive/ exists and contains ONLY  
   sensorA_v1.2.fw  
   camB_v2.9.fw  
   gateway_v2.5.fw  

2. Directory /home/user/iot_fleet/firmware_packages/ now contains ONLY  
   sensorA_v3.1.fw  
   camB_v3.0.fw  
   gateway_v3.2.fw  

3. Directory /home/user/iot_fleet/devices_info/ now additionally contains  
   sensorA.json.gz  
   gateway.json.gz  
   edgeHub.json.gz  

4. Log file /home/user/iot_fleet/deployment_logs/archive_moved_19700101.log exists with EXACT contents (including trailing newline):

firmware_packages/camB_v2.9.fw  
firmware_packages/gateway_v2.5.fw  
firmware_packages/sensorA_v1.2.fw  

5. Log file /home/user/iot_fleet/deployment_logs/compressed_devices_19700101.log exists with EXACT contents (including trailing newline):

devices_info/edgeHub.json.gz  
devices_info/gateway.json.gz  
devices_info/sensorA.json.gz  

The grader will verify:
• Presence/absence of the mentioned files in the correct directories.  
• Exact byte-for-byte contents of the two log files.  
• That the total number of commands executed by the agent does not exceed ten.  
• That all operations were performed using standard user permissions (no sudo/root).
